reviews.title: Hidden Charges and Shorter Days
reviews.text:
I was told that I could check-in early if I paid an early check-in fee of $100. I agreed to $100 but Sofitel actually charged my credit card $154!!!

I'm ok with NY State and City taxes but there was an "Urban Experience" charge for $34!!! What does this even mean???

So when Sofitel tries to charge you extra, make sure that extra doesn't have an additional 34% extra on top.

By the way, check-in is at 3pm and check-out is at 11am so instead of the usual 22 hours you're allowed to stay at other hotels, you just get 20 hours.

reviews.title: Holiday stay in the city a little underwhelming
reviews.text:
Had high expectations based on the research I did prior to selecting the Sofitel NY for our Christmas holiday stay in NYC, and the money I shelled out.

The positives: great location between Rockefeller Center, Times Square, and Grand Central Station; OK service; clean room; free WiFi; concierge Jeffrey. 

The negatives: small room, a bit aged, and lousy view from high floor; super slow elevators that tend to stop on every other floor going down without any people getting on; and $5 bottles of Evian on the bill because there was no complimentary bottled water in the room, even though we were certain the check-in attendant told us there was (regrettably I paid the charge when we checked out because I didn’t have time to argue).

I don’t feel like the value was up to the price I paid for the room. Maybe that’s the NYC holiday market, but there were plenty of Hilton hotels available in the area at two-thirds the price. I’d try one of those next time.

reviews.title: Good hotel

reviews.title: Lovely Hotel, Just Watch Out for the Talkative Bellman
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at the Sofitel in NYC and overall had a pleasant experience. The hotel is very clean, and the rooms are newly renovated and comfortable. The location is fantastic, making it easy to get around the city.

The only downside was encountering a somewhat odd bellman/door person. He was extremely talkative and took up quite a bit of our time, which was a bit inconvenient.

Overall, I will recommend the hotel for its comfort and location, but be prepared for some unusually long chats at the entrance!
reviews.response:
Bonjour Sijie,
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience at Sofitel New York. We’re delighted to hear that you enjoyed our newly renovated rooms, cleanliness, and especially our central location—it’s wonderful to know these elements made your stay comfortable and convenient.

We also appreciate your candid feedback regarding your interaction at the entrance. While our team strives to be welcoming and personable, we understand the importance of being mindful of our guests’ time. Your comments have been shared with our team so we can ensure our service strikes the right balance.

reviews.title: Hidden fees!! Beware!!
reviews.text: So I stayed at Sofitel for my birthday. The hotel itself is beautiful and so are the rooms. The staff was friendly enough. When I checked in I paid for the refundable deposit and the incidentals. That’s fine. When I checked out the woman at the desk said I had to pay an extra 33 resort fee. I was not made aware of that when I checked in. I paid it and left. I checked my card and I had been charged an extra 117 instead of 33!!!! I tried calling and no one picks up. While it’s a beautiful hotel I wouldn’t stay here again because of this. Unless you want to book a room and pay an extra 250 on top of it don’t stay here.

reviews.title: Decent stay but on to the next for us.
reviews.positives: Good location, clean and safe.
reviews.negatives: Lack of touch points with staff, no one provided us with insight on our "Urban Experience Fee" of $45 per night plus tax. This fee includes a $25 daily food and beverage credit, premium WiFi, a reusable water bottle, 24-hour fitness studio access, a 15% Bloomingdale's discount, and local/domestic calls. We had to go online to figure it out. Also we never received our water bottle. They don’t offer complimentary water in the room everything is for a fee. Ordering room service comes with the automatic 20%. There are a lot of fees and little is offered for the price point of this hotel. I’ve found that with higher price point hotels we are often provided more for our money. Also I am not sure they change the bedding often and we know this is a pet friendly hotel because our pillows smelled like dog.

reviews.title: It was generally decent/good.
reviews.positives: The renovated rooms, hot water for shower.
reviews.negatives:
No coffee maker or kettle which had to be asked for. Only bottles of water were provided and not replenished. No luggage rack/stand.
The steam machine was good but hard to steam anything because the power point was not near or in the closet and nothing to hang the clothes outside in the room to steam. The steam machine was good though. No handles outside of the closet to hang clothes on. The 2nd bathroom door almost seemed jammed and had to be pushed hard.

One more important thing was that for the two rooms I had booked, both were for high floors. However due to mine and the person checking us in lack of awareness, the room we stayed in was not a high floor and had a side building view. Luckily the second room my parents were in had a high view. The lady was kind of enough to at least look for rooms on the same floor (she was nice). But I dont know if I ended up paying more for a high floor room but didn’t get it because of the booking made through booking.com.

reviews.title: Lovely
reviews.text:

reviews.title: Great location with a little French flair
reviews.text: I enjoyed my stay at the hotel. I do find it bizarre however that at Sofitel hotels the world over they insist that everyone says bonjour whether they speak French or not. It is quite disconcerting. Whilst it is charming to hear bonjour from a French speaker, when Chinese maid says bonjour its doesn't quite have the same appeal. Here, I don't think I encountered a single French speaker. The food was good but the restaurant and bar doesn't have much charm in the evening. Music and lighting could improve this dramatically (a few candles perhaps if elf and safety will permit it). I would like to say that the staff were charming as I was genuinely quite excited to be staying in the hotel. However, the concierge was pretty grumpy and could have done with a stint at charm school. All in all however this is a good pick for visitors to the Big Apple and certainly better than many NYC hotels I have stayed in.

reviews.title: Great hotel at the right rate
reviews.text: We found a deal on Agoda that was significantly cheaper than booking direct. Although skeptical that the booking was going to be valid the reservation was seamless. The hotel is close to Times Square just a few minutes walk and located down a quiet street away from the hustle and bustle. The lobby is absolutely beautiful and we found all dealings with the staff pleasant, they were friendly and helpful. Although the hotel room is dated (looked the same as when I stayed there 20 years ago) it has been impeccably maintained and made for a comfortable stay, so I wouldn’t be to concerned about comments that the rooms are dated. The bed is showing its age and although I slept well it would be great to see the hotel invest in them to get them up to scratch with other Sofitels I have stayed in.  Strangely the fridge in the room was broken which appears to be common in this hotel after reading other reviews. Not really up to standard for a hotel of this level especially being in a one bedroom suite that can cost thousands to stay in and also missing was a kettle or coffee machine which was unusual, I can’t remember the last place I stayed that didn’t provide this. Overall due to the rate I managed to get I found value in my stay, if I paid the hotel rate I might have preferred another hotel. If you are looking for a hotel close to Times Square but in a quiet area I would  recommend staying here. Lastly although I’m aware of ‘destination fees’ in NYC hotels there was no mention of this on checking in and what benefits I could obtain, so I found absolutely no benefit to this during my stay.

reviews.title: A Week In New York
reviews.text: Our family enjoyed our stay at the Sofitel, occupying 3 rooms.  The staff was very friendly and accommodating.  Our rooms were spacious enough and had most of the standard amenities.   We did notice crumbs and a rubber band under the dresser in one room which indicated a lack of thorough vacuuming.  Otherwise, the rooms were clean and the beds comfortable.    Surprisingly, there was no ice, coffee or water available in or near the room.   We quickly learned to purchase water on our excursions.   We had room service the night of our arrival and ate breakfast in the downstairs restaurant once which was delicious. although a little pricey.  Location is great...walkable to many key tourist interests.  Being an older hotel, the electricity was a little tricky...an outlet that didn't work, turning on a lamp and the other lamps turn off...nothing major.  Overall in spite of the few road bumps, we had a positive experience and enjoyed our stay at the NY Sofitel Hotel.

reviews.title: Small room, high price, good location.
reviews.positives: Upon booking, I had asked for a quiet room, and we were given a room on the 7th floor at the back. It has to be the quietest room in NY that I`ve ever stayed in. But VERY small for the money!  Location was just off Times Square, so central, but you`re away from the noise. Bed was comfy.
reviews.negatives: The room size was a joke, really, it was nothing more than a `box room`.  You may say, `Well, why didn`t you complain?`.  My answer to that would be that if my custom is valued, I should have been given a better room in the first place.  Considering the price we paid, the fact that we were booked in for 7 nights, and as a member of the Accor scheme for some time, we expected a lot more.  Maybe next time we book with Accor, this will show on your system? But I doubt it, this is a box-ticking excercise, isn`t it? Hopefully not, and our comments will mean something to you all, as you operate in a competitive market, where I, as the customer, have the choice as to where I stay.

reviews.title: 3 nights - fantastic
reviews.text:
We moved here from another NYC hotel and experienced an upgrade in location, housekeeping and it was more luxurious. We booked a superior twin room and it was roomy enough to open up our suitcases and still move about comfortably. Not much of a view from our 7th floor window, but being between 5th and 6th avenues made it easy to get around and find lots of food and shopping options within walking distance.

We arrived after checking out of our earlier hotel and to our delight the room was ready for us before check in time. Everything was clean and working. Small problematic area from wear and tear (the toilet door panels were  sticking slightly) and there were no direct USB A ports. But hardly noticeable and did not affect the overall comfort of the stay

Beds are on the soft side; would’ve preferred more support. 

Towels and linen were great, plush and clean. Towels were replaced daily even when we hung them up for reuse. 

Amenities provided included cotton buds, cotton pads and shower cap, and we loved the bedroom slippers! Nicer than the thin disposable ones hotels usually give. We took ours home with us (as encouraged by the hotel). No bottled water, no toothbrushes (latter available on request, I think)

AC worked well and was quiet. The windows were always shut and double walled but some traffic noise still comes through, but it was not too bad. 

We would stay here again if we visit NYC!

reviews.title: A hotel that is well-located, safe, clean, and spacious.
reviews.text:
I stayed with my mom at the Sofitel in New York for nine nights. I wanted to avoid the hassle of packing and moving around. In conclusion, it was a great choice! The bed was really comfortable and the bedding was cozy, so I slept well. Even though it is in the expensive area of Manhattan, the room was spacious enough that we could open our suitcase, and it had a bathtub as well as a large and luxurious bathroom!

Within a 5-minute walk, you can reach Bryant Park, Whole Foods Market, and Times Square.

The bus terminal at Port Authority is also within walking distance when going to Woodbury Outlet, which is convenient.

It took about 20 minutes to walk to Central Park. I felt it was a waste of money to take the bus or subway, but walking was quite a distance.

When walking to MoMA (Museum of Modern Art) or the nearby Halal Guys from the hotel, it took about 20 minutes. However, there were many things to see along the way, so it was worth walking. There’s Magnolia Bakery on the way and many parks, so it's nice to grab Halal Guys on the way.

There were two disappointing points. 
First, there was the urban fee? I’m not sure if other hotels also charge this, but it was about 48,000 won per day, so I ended up paying an additional 480,000 won at checkout, excluding the amount I had already paid.

Also, the deposit was quite high. I ended up being charged about 1.5 million won, which was a bit surprising even though it would be refunded later.

The second disappointing point was, although it might be coincidental, when I checked in and went into the room, there was no water. They said they would bring water if I asked, and I couldn’t help but wonder if they did this to possibly receive a tip? I may be wrong, but that was the feeling I got.

In any case, apart from that, I found the location very good, the hotel was pleasant, and I felt relatively safe even at night, so I would recommend it.

reviews.text:
Sofitel's bedding is overwhelming. It was so nice that I almost asked the desk, "Where can I buy the pillows and blankets?"

The desk staff was very friendly, and even though we arrived early, they let us check in about 2 hours ahead of time. It felt like a hotel with many guests.

I had no discomfort during my stay. The only downside was that laundry was really expensive, so I hand-washed my clothes.

And crucially, I was told that if you leave something behind somewhere else, you should really expect to lose it, but here I left my expensive camera and earrings, and housekeeping found them all for me... Really. The service is top-notch.

We are visiting again in August and will stay here again. It's much better than Paramount. The location is also really good. There's a Walmart very close by. I highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Awesomest staff ever
reviews.text: So what had happened was, we booked through Agoda and for some reason, the hotel didnt have our booking. However they did have some way to show our payment was accepted and approved. We went to check in and as stated, we got a king sized room. However unlike what was told during our phone call with staff AS WELL AS what was expressed on agodas booking page for the room, it was not what was shown. The staff of the hotel had explained to us what their version of a king sized meant as opposed to what was posted on agoda (so please call in to double check). When we told the staff member at the front desk about the mixup of expectations, he immediately found a response involving his manager and other coworker. I just want to whole heartedly say, thank you sir Gilbert for not only saving the night but changing our lives forever. The hospitality, kindness, professionalism and understanding was so unique that this review had to be written. Sir Gilbert along with his coworkers and manager were put in a predicament that i doubt many would be able to navigate but he did it with class and a smile. When we told him that the room was meant for us to have an intimate wedding, he found ways to upgrade our room to the very top that they had to offer. We recieved complimentary wine and a beautiful letter signed by the staff that made our special night, truly one to remember. When doing our photos, the staff allowed us to take shots in areas that were secluded for a more peaceful and elegant effect. To say that they went above and beyond all expectations was nothing short of a miracle.  And my goodness the skyscraper rooms really are the top of the top at the heart of manhattan. You can see everything! Not to mention the breakfast that was complimentary at the original booking was still honored, it was delicious! My now wife and i are thinking about staying again. It was a beautiful night. They made our night, spectacular.
